briefing.headline: Rig up a Walking Topwater — steady post-spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Walking Topwater is a strong choice for post-spawn with stained water and stable conditions.

Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC hooks get into the fish

Key adjustment for stable: moderate cadence — match the natural pace of local forage.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use monofilament (Sufix Siege) for topwater fishing — the stretch prevents jerking the bait away from the fish on the hookset. Pull into the fish rather than jerking, letting the sharp VMC ho
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In Florida heavy grass, switch to 30-lb braid with a 12-inch monofilament leader (Sufix Elite) for topwater — need to force fish out of hay-field grass or you'll lose them
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, creek channel, bluff. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Hackney: 'Especially in the South, females like to bunch up after spawning. When you find one female, you find a group of them. Use topwater around those groups as females seem to like it more.'

Wheeler: Post-spawn offshore on Table Rock, the 3 key elements were brushpiles, isolated cedar trees, and bluff end points/big long main-lake points where schools related to current and hard bottom — mixed species (smallmouth, largemouth, spotted ...

KVD: Clear water on grass lakes = fish can see the bait from farther away (benefit) but can also be spooky (challenge). Fish may be timid — bites are subtle, just bumping the bait. Slow down and keep working the area.

briefing.depthStrategy: Stable post-spawn conditions split fish between shallow fry-guarders (2-5ft) and transitioning females (5-15ft). Shad spawn activity on seawalls, riprap, and bluff walls concentrates bigger fish shallow at dawn.
adjustIf: If conditions suddenly shift (pressure drop, wind change), be ready to speed up your approach — transitional moments produce big bites
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If fish are blowing up but not connecting, pause 3-5 seconds after each strike before resuming
